Overnight sleep; ends before 6am as required, spanning the night boundary naturally.

diary: Woke up late but made it work—instant ramen and an energy drink, then rushed over to Solstice to catch the USA vs Wales match with some people from my dorm. The place was packed, everyone losing it during the game. Grabbed a slice too, which hit different when you're surrounded by that kind of energy. After that, I knew I had to knuckle down at the library. The Light Rail being closed didn't really affect me since I'm just walking everywhere in the U-District anyway. Hit up the courts around 2:30 for some pickup basketball—needed to burn off some steam after staring at CS problem sets. My legs were toast by the time I got ramen at Taneda, but it was worth it. Came back to the library for another solid study push until almost 8. By the time I got home, I was completely wiped. Just scrolled for a bit before crashing. Days like this feel full in a weird way—bouncing between things, never quite settling.
thought_process: I structured my day around momentum: the World Cup match was a built-in social thing I didn't want to miss, and it got me out of the house early despite sleeping in. The Light Rail closure didn't matter since I'm always on foot in U-District anyway. I balanced study time with basketball because I knew if I just sat in the library all day, I'd burn out mentally. The ramen stops were strategic—cheap refuels that kept me functional. I chose to go back to the library instead of calling it quits because I had assignments due soon, and the afternoon study session felt more productive than the morning one. By evening I was running on fumes, so the gaming wind-down felt earned rather than like procrastination. The sunny weather probably helped my mood too—made walking everywhere feel less like a chore.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
